title: Cannot use ARG variables in the RUN --mount=type=bind values
body:
### Issue Description

It looks like the following Docker feature is missing in Podman: https://github.com/moby/buildkit/issues/815

I cannot use ARG variables in the RUN --mount=type=bind values.
They work in other places, but not in --mount=type=bind values. Manually replacing $RELATIVE_PATH with hardcoded value makes it work. But I need ARG.

### Steps to reproduce the issue

Steps to reproduce the issue
```
ARG RELATIVE_PATH=.

RUN echo /app/$RELATIVE_PATH

RUN ls -la /app/$RELATIVE_PATH

RUN \
    --mount=type=cache,target=/root/.cache/uv \
    --mount=type=bind,source=$RELATIVE_PATH/uv.lock,target=uv.lock,relabel=shared \
    --mount=type=bind,source=$RELATIVE_PATH/pyproject.toml,target=pyproject.toml,relabel=shared \
    --mount=type=bind,source=$RELATIVE_PATH/.python-version,target=.python-version,relabel=shared \
    ls -la /app/$RELATIVE_PATH
```

### Describe the results you received

```
STEP 8/12: RUN     --mount=type=cache,target=/root/.cache/uv     --mount=type=bind,source=$RELATIVE_PATH/uv.lock,target=uv.lock,relabel=shared     --mount=type=bind,source=$RELATIVE_PATH/pyproject.toml,target=pyproject.toml,relabel=shared     --mount=type=bind,source=$RELATIVE_PATH/.python-version,target=.python-version,relabel=shared     ls -la /app/$RELATIVE_PATH
Error: checking if cached image exists from a previous build: unable to get createdBy for the node: generating checksum for directory "$RELATIVE_PATH/uv.lock" in "/var/tmp/libpod_builder1125775824/build": lstat /var/tmp/libpod_builder1125775824/build/$RELATIVE_PATH/uv.lock: no such file or directory
```

When using hardcoded values, it works:

```
STEP 8/12: RUN     --mount=type=cache,target=/root/.cache/uv     --mount=type=bind,source=XXX/uv.lock,target=uv.lock,relabel=shared     --mount=type=bind,source=XXX/pyproject.toml,target=pyproject.toml,relabel=shared     --mount=type=bind,source=XXX/.python-version,target=.python-version,relabel=shared     ls -la /app/$RELATIVE_PATH
total 36
drwxr-xr-x. 1 root root    66 May  1 00:55 .
drwxr-xr-x. 1 root root    30 May  1 00:54 ..
-rw-r--r--. 1 root root     8 May  1 00:45 .python-version
-rw-r--r--. 1 root root   187 May  1 00:45 pyproject.toml
-rw-r--r--. 1 root root 28099 May  1 00:45 uv.lock
```

### Describe the results you expected

I expect ARG variables to work in the RUN --mount=type=bind values

### podman info output

### Privileged Or Rootless

### Upstream Latest Release

### Additional information

comments.body:
Hi @Ark-kun 

I can reproduce this, but I will have to defer to someone else on if this is expected or a bug. 

The reason I'm not sure is because at least your use case seems to be the wrong way to do it. 

When building a container a "context" is involved, for example `podman build -f Containerfile $HOME/src/files`. We can see from the podman build help that '$HOME/src/files' will be used as the context for the build process. [1]

So normally you put the Containerfile at the root of your project and from the root of the project you run `podman build -f Containerfile .`, this would set the relative path to your current directory. 

If you had a layout like the following than from the root of the project you could run `podman build -f containerfiles/Dockerfile ./code`. Any files you reference in the container file will have their path prepended with the `code` directory path. :

```
$ tree
.
├── compose.yml
├── containerfiles
│   └── Dockerfile
├── README.md
├── code
│   └── src
│       └── uv_docker_example
│           └── __init__.py
```

Let me know what you think. 

If the other maintainers believe this is a bug or missing feature they will likely move this issue to the [Buildah project](https://github.com/containers/buildah)

@Luap99 @mheon could you please confirm if you have time

--- 

[1] `podman build [options] [CONTEXT]`

A reviewer has determined we need more information to understand the reported issue.  A comment on what is missing should be provided. Be certain you:

* provide an exact reproducer where possible
* verify you have provided all relevant information - minimum is `podman info`
* answer any follow up questions

If no response to the `needs-info` is provided in 30 days, this issue may be closed by our stale bot.

For more information on reporting issues on this repository, consult our [issue guide](ISSUE.md).

@ninja-quokka Thank you for the explanation and guidance.
I still think my use scenario is valid: I working with a Git semi-mono-repo with lots of Python code and many Python scripts in different places. I need to make `Dockerfile`s for those scripts, but the scripts rely on shared code outside of their directory. This is why I need to include the repo root in the context. But the packages I need to install and programs I need to run are different for each script.

Basically, I want to make a single `Dockerfile` that I can use for all these scripts by just specifying the `RELATIVE_PATH` arg.

```
─── repo-root
    ├── .git
    ├── Dockerfile.with_relative_path_support
    ├── libraries
    │   └── library 1
    │       └── module.py
    └── scripts
        ├── script1
        │   ├── Dockerfile
        │   ├── main.py
        │   ├── pyproject.toml
        │   └── uv.lock
        └── script2
            ├── Dockerfile
            ├── main.py
            ├── pyproject.toml
            └── uv.lock
```
This is fixed in upstream via https://github.com/containers/buildah/pull/6110
This should be fixed in newer releases of podman.
